mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series grow, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, but this mechanism only generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.5 percent.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not materialize when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the regime-conditional alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and consumer spending expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll gains exceeding 100,000, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 0.35 percent, or the predictive coefficient flips sign in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand that lifts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth decelerates while RSAFS accelerates, or the joint predictor yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal weakening manufacturing capacity utilization that depresses equity valuations, but this negative effect only materializes over 5 trading days when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to occur when INDPRO falls and UNRATE is above 5.0 percent, or the regime-specific predictive power drops below a Rank IC of 0.02 in rolling windows.
